title: Crataegus phaenopyrum
taxonomy: Crataegus phaenopyrum is classified within the family Rosaceae. This family includes various species of flowering plants such as roses and apples. Crataegus phaenopyrum is further classified in the genus Crataegus, which comprises hawthorn trees. The genus Crataegus is divided into several species, with Crataegus phaenopyrum being one of them. It is native to Asia and is cultivated in other parts of the world for its ornamental value.
uses: Crataegus phaenopyrum, also known as the Washington hawthorn, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves, flowers, and fruits are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Traditionally, it has been used to treat conditions such as high blood pressure, fever, and digestive issues. Some studies suggest that the extracts of the Washington hawthorn may have cardiovascular benefits and help to reduce the risk of heart disease. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of this plant.
benefits: Crataegus phaenopyrum, also known as Washington hawthorn, is a shrub that is native to China and has been used in traditional medicine. The plant is rich in flavonoids, which are known for their antioxidant properties and potential health benefits. Studies have suggested that extracts from the Washington hawthorn may help to lower cholesterol levels and reduce blood pressure. It is believed to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which may help to reduce blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties, including cardiotonic and vasodilatory effects. constituents: Crataegus phaenopyrum, also known as the Washington hawthorn, contains a range of active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids. These compounds have been reported to have various biological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. side_effects: Crataegus phaenopyrum, also known as the Washington hawthorn, is a species of flowering plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. intro: Crataegus phaenopyrum, also known as the Washington hawthorn, is a deciduous tree native to eastern Asia that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including cardiovascular issues, due to its potential health benefits such as lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The bioactive constituents of Crataegus phaenopyrum include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and oligostilbenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Crataegus phaenopyrum include standardized extracts, capsules, and teas, which are often used to support heart health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
